scenario: Draft a plaint for recovery of Rs.15,00,000 given as a hand loan. The plaintiff Ram Kumar, aged 45, resident of Jayanagar, Bengaluru, advanced Rs.15,00,000 to the defendant Suresh Patel, aged 38, resident of Koramangala, Bengaluru, on 15.03.2024 via NEFT bank transfer (UTR: AXIB20240315123456). There was no written agreement. The defendant acknowledged the loan via WhatsApp messages. Despite repeated oral demands and a legal notice dated 01.07.2024, the defendant has failed to repay. The plaintiff claims interest at 12% per annum from the date of default (20.06.2024). File in City Civil Court, Bengaluru.

scorecard.detail: At least one statutory section cited

draft_text:
IN THE CITY CIVIL COURT AT BENGALURU (KARNATAKA)

O.S. No. {{SUIT_NUMBER}} of 2024

BETWEEN:

RAM KUMAR,
Aged about 45 years,
{{OCCUPATION}},
S/o {{FATHER_NAME}},
R/o Jayanagar, Bengaluru - {{PIN_CODE}}. 
... PLAINTIFF

AND:

SURESH PATEL,
Aged about 38 years,
{{OCCUPATION}},
S/o {{FATHER_NAME}},
R/o Koramangala, Bengaluru - {{PIN_CODE}}.
... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR RECOVERY OF MONEY

FACTS

1. The Plaintiff and the Defendant are acquainted.

2. On 15.03.2024, the Plaintiff advanced a sum of Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs only) to the Defendant as a hand loan. The amount was transferred via NEFT bank transfer bearing UTR Number AXIB20240315123456.

3. There was no written agreement executed between the parties in respect of the said loan. The Defendant acknowledged the receipt of the loan amount and the liability to repay the same through WhatsApp messages exchanged between the parties.

4. The Defendant failed to repay the said amount. The Plaintiff treats 20.06.2024 as the date of default for repayment and claims interest at 12% per annum from 20.06.2024.

5. Despite repeated oral demands, the Defendant has failed and neglected to repay the outstanding amount.

6. The Plaintiff caused a legal notice dated 01.07.2024 to be issued to the Defendant calling upon him to repay the outstanding dues. Despite the said notice, the Defendant has failed to pay.

7. To the best of the Plaintiff’s knowledge and belief, the Defendant has not made any part-payment towards the principal amount or interest. The amount outstanding as on the date of filing is Rs. 15,00,000/- together with interest as claimed herein.

8. The Defendant’s non-payment has caused loss to the Plaintiff.

LEGAL_BASIS

9. The Plaintiff relies upon Section 73 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872.

10. The Plaintiff relies upon Section 20 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908 for territorial jurisdiction.

CAUSE OF ACTION

11. The cause of action arose on 15.03.2024 when the Plaintiff advanced the loan to the Defendant and the Defendant became liable to repay the same. The cause of action further arose on 20.06.2024 when the Defendant defaulted in repayment, and again on 01.07.2024 when the Plaintiff issued a legal notice demanding repayment. The cause of action arose within Bengaluru where the Defendant resides and where the transaction took place. The cause of action continues as the Defendant has not repaid.

LIMITATION

12. The suit is governed by Article 19 of the Limitation Act, 1963 (money payable for money lent). The period of limitation is three years and the limitation is computed from 15.03.2024. The present suit is within limitation.

JURISDICTION

13. This Hon’ble Court has territorial jurisdiction as the Defendant resides within Bengaluru and the cause of action arose within Bengaluru, within the meaning of Section 20 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908. This Hon’ble Court has pecuniary jurisdiction as the suit valuation is within its pecuniary limits.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

14. For the purposes of pecuniary jurisdiction and court fee, the suit is valued at Rs. 15,00,000/- being the principal amount claimed. Court fee of Rs. {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}} is paid herewith under the Karnataka Court Fees and Suits Valuation Act, 1958.

INTEREST

15. The Plaintiff claims pre-suit interest at the rate of 12% per annum from 20.06.2024 till the date of filing of the plaint. The Plaintiff also seeks pendente lite and future interest under Section 34 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908.

PRAYER

The Plaintiff prays that this Hon’ble Court be pleased to pass a judgment and decree in favour of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant as follows:

(a) Directing the Defendant to pay Rs. 15,00,000/- being the principal amount of the loan;

(b) Granting pre-suit interest at 12% per annum from 20.06.2024 till the date of filing;

(c) Granting pendente lite interest at such rate as this Hon’ble Court deems fit from the date of filing of the suit till the date of decree under Section 34 CPC;

(d) Granting future interest at such rate as this Hon’ble Court deems fit from the date of decree till realization under Section 34 CPC;

(e) Awarding costs of the suit under Section 35 CPC; and

(f) Granting such other reliefs as this Hon’ble Court deems fit in the facts and circumstances of the case.

LIST OF DOCUMENTS (ANNEXURES)

P-1: Bank transaction record showing NEFT transfer details with UTR: AXIB20240315123456 dated 15.03.2024.

P-2: Printouts of WhatsApp messages acknowledging the loan.

P-3: Copy of legal notice dated 01.07.2024.

VERIFICATION

I, Ram Kumar, the Plaintiff above named, do hereby verify that the contents of paragraphs 1 to 8 and 11 to 15 are true and correct to my personal knowledge and belief, and that the contents of paragraphs 9 to 10 are based on legal advice which I believe to be true. Verified at Bengaluru on this {{DATE}} day of {{MONTH}}, 2024.

PLACE: BENGALURU
DATE: {{DATE}}

DEPONENT

(RAM KUMAR)

ADVOCATE BLOCK

COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF
{
